patch.value: pm next: recommend the next actionable (unblocked, ready) work item with rationale + blocked companion

Close the single biggest agent-ergonomics gap in the read surface: there is no command that answers 'what should I work on next?'. `pm context` shows a rich snapshot (hierarchy/progress/workload/blockers) and ranks active items via compareCriticalItems, but it never computes dependency-aware READINESS — items with open blocked_by dependencies still appear in the focus lists, and nothing distills a single recommended next action. This forces every agent to re-derive readiness from the snapshot on each turn, burning context. `pm next` is the distilled context-management primitive: one call -> the one item to do now + why, the ranked ready queue, and the blocked queue with its blockers. Reuses runList (corpus), compareCriticalItems (ranking), and the blocked_by resolver (shared with close.ts auto-unblock); pure readiness/classification logic extracted into a small core module (src/core/item/actionability.ts) per ADR pm-7sq6.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Adding a new core command to PM_TOOL_ACTIONS (for MCP pm_run) WITHOUT a matching entry in PM_TOOL_ACTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS crashes `pm contracts --full --json` (and thus `contracts:check`) with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading required)' in buildActionScopedToolSchema — the per-action MCP schema builder indexes that map by action. PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES (CLI/completion) and PM_TOOL_ACTIONS (MCP) are independent: `eval` is CLI-only (in NAMES, not ACTIONS); `next` is in BOTH. The c8 single-line `c8 ignore next N` did NOT suppress branch coverage on a ternary/if-return defensive line under @vitest/coverage-v8 — only the `c8 ignore start/stop` block form works (matches context.ts convention). Reverse-graph 'unblocks' dependents are always corpus items, so a `dependent !== undefined` guard is dead code that breaks 100% branch cov — filter against a precomputed non-terminal id Set instead.

patch.value: `pm next` (CLI + MCP pm_run action + narrow pm_next tool) returns a recommended ready item with rationale, a ranked ready queue, and a blocked companion queue; dependency-aware readiness reuses the shared blocked_by resolver and compareCriticalItems; 100% test + docstring coverage; contracts/completion/docs updated.

Shipped. New core/item/actionability.ts (collectBlockedByIds extracted from close.ts as the single source of truth; resolveItemBlockers; computeActionabilityReport partitioning ready/blocked/containers). New cli/commands/next.ts (runNext + renderNextMarkdown + resolveNextOutputFormat). Promoted compareCriticalItems/collectSubtreeIds/toContextFocusItem/buildChildrenByParent to documented context.ts exports. Wired register-list-query (next command), enum-contracts (PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES + PM_TOOL_ACTIONS), cli-contracts (NEXT_FLAG_CONTRACTS + resolver case + NEXT_CONTRACT_PARAMETER_KEYS + PM_TOOL_ACTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.next), contracts.ts map, completion bash/zsh/fish, help-content, MCP server (case next + pm_next handler + tool def + instructions), tool-parameter-tables (blockedLimit/readyOnly). Verified in temp dir (10/10 readiness scenarios) + 5041 tests 100/100/100/100 coverage + static & docs-skills gates green + contracts snapshot regenerated.

Delivered `pm next` end-to-end: new core/item/actionability.ts (shared blocked_by resolver + ready/blocked/container classification), cli/commands/next.ts (runNext + markdown renderer), CLI registration, full contracts/completion/help wiring, MCP pm_run action + narrow pm_next tool, and COMMANDS/AGENT_GUIDE/README docs. 5041 tests at 100/100/100/100 coverage; static + docs-skills gates green; contracts snapshot regenerated; verified in a temp dir (10/10 readiness scenarios). ADR pm-9x6k.

Shipped `pm next` as a first-class read primitive: core/item/actionability.ts (shared blocked_by resolver + ready/blocked/container classification), cli/commands/next.ts, full CLI/contracts/completion/help wiring, MCP pm_run action + narrow pm_next tool, and docs. 100/100/100/100 coverage across 5041 tests; verified in a temp dir.
